---
title: "Teaching"
---

## Courses

### Epi 204: Quantitative Epidemiology III: Statistical Models

Lecture notes and materials for Epi 204 are available on GitHub:

- [Epi 204 Lecture Notes Repository](https://d-morrison.github.io/rme/)

[View Teaching Evaluations](evals.qmd)

#### Student Feedback Highlights

```{r}
#| label: epi204-comment-highlights
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| results: asis

source("data/extract_evals.R", local = TRUE)
rds_path <- "data/evals_data.rds"
if (file.exists(rds_path)) {
  evals_data <- readRDS(rds_path)
  comments <- evals_data$comments
  if (!is.null(comments) && nrow(comments) > 0) {
    # Restrict to Epi 204 (this page is the Epi 204 section), then keep
    # only unambiguously positive feedback. See is_positive_comment() in
    # data/extract_evals.R for the full cue list.
    comments <- comments[comments$course == "Epi 204", ]
    positive <- comments[vapply(
      seq_len(nrow(comments)),
      function(i) is_positive_comment(comments$comment[i], comments$role[i]),
      logical(1)
    ), ]
    if (nrow(positive) > 0) {
      by_year <- split(positive, positive$year)
      by_year <- by_year[order(as.integer(names(by_year)), decreasing = TRUE)]
      for (g in by_year) {
        label <- sprintf("%s", g$year[[1]])
        cat(sprintf("::: {.callout-note icon=false title=\"%s\"}\n", label))
        for (cmt in g$comment) cat("- ", cmt, "\n", sep = "")
        cat(":::\n\n")
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Presentations

### Introduction to Causal Inference

Douglas Ezra Morrison, Ph.D. – January 19, 2022

<a href="https://health.ucdavis.edu/media-resources/ctsc/documents/pdfs/causal-inference-intro-2022.pdf">Presentation PDF</a> | <a href="https://ucdhs.hosted.panopto.com/Panopto/Pages/Viewer.aspx?id=43e9eb6f-3ed9-41ac-8ad9-ae22016572c8">Video</a> (42:18)
